name: my-dev-team
description: An agentic chat participant for VS Code with tool-calling (read, search, run, write) and built-in feedback.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Triage, oneshot answers, /explain, and the read/search tools work in an untrusted folder; the side-effecting tools (run, write, edit) are disabled and MCP servers are not contacted until you trust the workspace.

virtualWorkspaces.description: Reading, searching, writing, and editing files work in a virtual workspace; the run tool needs a local filesystem and is disabled there.

enumDescriptions: Run the agent pipeline inside the extension against your local Ollama server.

Run the same pipeline in a separate Node process (a sidecar). The tools, approval, and rendering stay in the editor; the engine is isolated. Cloud API keys are read from environment variables, which the child inherits.
Not available yet: will run the same pipeline on a remote backend. Falls back to local with a warning.
myDevTeam.engine.description: Which engine handles @devteam requests. Read live; switching takes effect on the next request.

myDevTeam.model.description: What @devteam uses for the planner, answerer, and executor: a model id, a "provider:<name>" to route among one provider's models (best per task), or "auto" to let the router pick the best available model per agent. The quick triage step is configured separately by myDevTeam.triage.model. Use the /model command or the status-bar item to change it from a list.

myDevTeam.triage.model.description: What @devteam uses for the quick internal triage step (deciding whether a request is a question or work). Leave empty to use the build's default (a local Ollama model unless the build changed it). Set it when you have no Ollama server: "provider:openai" (or anthropic/groq) routes within that provider, "auto" picks the best available model, or a model id pins one. A provider or model the build disabled cannot be selected here.

myDevTeam.complexityRouting.description: Size the executor's model to how demanding triage judges the request: simpler work routes to a cheaper/smaller model, harder work to a stronger one (within whatever provider applies). Turn off to route the executor by capability alone. A pinned model is never affected.

enumDescriptions: Pause for approval only when the planner judges the work complex.
Pause for approval after every drafted plan.
Never pause: run drafted plans straight through (the pre-gate behaviour).
myDevTeam.planApproval.description: When @devteam pauses to let you approve a drafted plan before it runs. At the gate you can Approve (execute), Cancel (keep the plan, run nothing), or Revise (type a comment and have the plan redrafted).

myDevTeam.disabledProviders.description: Providers @devteam must never use (e.g. ["anthropic"]). The router skips their models and the /model picker shows them as disabled, even if an API key is set. A disabled provider never runs even when pinned. This is your personal layer on top of any providers the build itself disables, which you cannot re-enable here.

myDevTeam.disabledModels.description: Individual model ids @devteam must never use (e.g. ["qwen3-coder"]). The router skips them and the /model picker shows them as disabled; a disabled model never runs even when pinned (the run falls back to Auto). On top of any models the build itself disables, which you cannot re-enable here.

myDevTeam.ollama.endpoint.description: Base URL of the Ollama server (origin only, no /api suffix). Leave blank to use the server your deployment ships with, falling back to http://localhost:11434; set a value here to point at your own server (your setting wins over the deployment default). Model calls, error hints, and the startup health check all use the resolved value.

myDevTeam.openai.baseUrl.description: Optional custom base URL for the OpenAI provider (an Azure or OpenAI-compatible gateway). Leave empty to use OpenAI's default endpoint. The API key is read from the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable (or, for the local engine, the "Set API Key" command), not here.

myDevTeam.anthropic.baseUrl.description: Optional custom base URL for the Anthropic provider (a proxy or gateway). Leave empty to use Anthropic's default endpoint. The API key is read from the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable (or, for the local engine, the "Set API Key" command), not here.

myDevTeam.groq.baseUrl.description: Optional custom base URL for the Groq provider (a proxy or gateway). Leave empty to use Groq's default endpoint. The API key is read from the GROQ_API_KEY environment variable (or, for the local engine, the "Set API Key" command), not here.

myDevTeam.provider.requestsPerMinute.description: Your override for the maximum model requests per minute sent to each provider. Requests are spaced so no provider exceeds this rate, keeping a run under its quota (e.g. a Groq free-tier limit); applied per provider, so a local Ollama call never spends a cloud provider's budget. Leave unset (null) to use the per-provider rate the deployment ships with; set a number to override it (raise or lower), or 0 to disable throttling. A rate-limited (HTTP 429) request is always retried after the delay the provider suggests, regardless of this setting.

myDevTeam.run.commandTimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds for shell commands started by the run tool. The whole process tree is killed when it expires.

myDevTeam.read.maxLines.description: Maximum number of lines the read tool returns per call. A partial result names the range shown and the file's total lines so the model can continue.

myDevTeam.search.globMaxResults.description: Maximum number of files a glob search returns.

myDevTeam.search.contentScanLimit.description: Maximum number of files a content search scans.

myDevTeam.search.contentMaxMatches.description: Maximum number of matches a content search collects before stopping early.

myDevTeam.chat.toolSnippetLines.description: Number of leading lines of a written file's contents shown as a snippet under the write call in the execution transcript. 0 hides the snippet.

myDevTeam.instructions.files.description: Project instruction files probed in the workspace root, in order; the first one found is given to the agents as standing instructions on every request. Plain file names only (no folders). An empty list turns the feature off.

myDevTeam.skills.directories.description: Root-relative directories scanned for skills, each at <dir>/<name>/SKILL.md. A skill is a named, described block of instructions the agent loads on demand when a task matches its description (built-in skills ship with the extension regardless). An empty list turns workspace skills off; an entry that is absolute or contains .. makes the whole list fall back to the default.

command.description: The executable to launch the MCP server (e.g. "npx").

env.description: Environment variables for the server process.

myDevTeam.mcp.servers.markdownDescription: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers whose tools @devteam may call, as a name -> definition map, e.g. `{ "filesystem": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "."] } }`. Each server is launched over stdio; its tools are offered to the model namespaced as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`, and every call is approved through the same prompt the run tool uses. Server names must be plain identifiers (letters, digits, _ , -); invalid entries are ignored. No server is contacted in an untrusted workspace. New or changed servers take effect on a window reload.

myDevTeam.write.protectedPaths.description: Root-relative locations the write and edit tools refuse to touch, on top of the always-protected .git/ (these can run code automatically, e.g. VS Code tasks). Matched per path segment, so .git does not catch .gitignore. An empty list keeps only .git protected; an entry with .. makes the whole list fall back to the default.

myDevTeam.approval.fileChanges.description: Require approval before the write and edit tools change a file. Off by default: changes apply immediately, since a git-backed workspace makes them recoverable. Turn this on to confirm every write and edit with the same Approve/Decline prompt the run tool uses (the run tool is always gated, regardless of this setting). Read live; takes effect on the next request.

myDevTeam.usage.showInChat.description: Append a Tokens line under each reply summing the run's input and output tokens. The status-bar session total and the My Dev Team: Show Token Usage report are independent of this setting.

myDevTeam.changes.showInChat.description: Append a Changes line under a reply that wrote files, summing them as "N files changed, +X -Y". The line only appears when a turn actually changed files.

myDevTeam.summary.showInChat.description: After an executed plan that changed files, add a Summary recap in three sections (What ships, How it's built, Tests and docs). Turning this off skips the extra summarizer model call entirely.

myDevTeam.thinking.showInChat.description: Show a reasoning model's thinking live as a transient progress line while @devteam works, condensed to its latest line. The thinking is never kept past the run; turning this off skips capturing reasoning entirely.

myDevTeam.telemetry.evalLog.description: Store a local eval log of @devteam runs (route, per-step model and token usage, outcome) and thumbs up/down feedback as JSON lines in the extension's global storage. No prompts, file contents, or replies are recorded, and nothing leaves your machine. The My Dev Team: Show Token Usage report reads this log.

myDevTeam.telemetry.shadowTriage.description: On a slash-command run (which pins the route), also run triage in the background to record what it would have decided, so the Show Token Usage report can score triage against the command you chose. Adds one local triage call per pinned run and only collects data while the eval log is on. Off by default.

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Start fresh: drop the conversation so far from future requests.

commands.name: model
commands.description: Choose the model @devteam uses (or Auto to route by capability).

commands.name: compact
commands.description: Summarize the conversation so far; the summary then stands in for it.

commands.name: do
commands.description: Plan and execute workspace changes - no triage.

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain a file, selection, or concept in the chat - no changes made.

commands.name: fix
commands.description: Diagnose a bug, fix its root cause, and verify the fix.

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Draft the step-by-step plan but do not execute it.

commands.name: review
commands.description: Review the attached code or selection - findings in chat, no edits.

commands.name: test
commands.description: Write or update tests for the target code, then run them.

languageModelTools.name: devteam__read
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Read the text of a workspace file given its relative path, up to a configured number of lines per call. Optional startLine/endLine select a 1-based inclusive range; a partial result names the range shown, the file's total lines, and the startLine to continue with.

path.description: Workspace-relative path of the file to read.

startLine.description: First line to read (1-based, inclusive). Defaults to 1.

endLine.description: Last line to read (1-based, inclusive). Defaults to the per-call line cap counted from startLine; clamped to it when larger.

languageModelTools.name: devteam__search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the workspace for files matching a glob, or for text contained within files.

query.description: Glob pattern (e.g. **/*.ts) or text to search for.

mode.description: Whether to match file names by glob or search file contents for text.

languageModelTools.name: devteam__run
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Run a shell command in the workspace root. This is a side-effecting action and requires user confirmation.

languageModelTools.name: devteam__write
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create or overwrite a file in the workspace with the given contents. This is a side-effecting action and requires user confirmation.

path.description: Workspace-relative path of the file to create or update.

contents.description: The full new contents of the file.

languageModelTools.name: devteam__edit
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Replace text in an existing workspace file. The exact old text must match one place in the file. This is a side-effecting action and requires user confirmation.

path.description: Workspace-relative path of the existing file to edit.

oldText.description: The exact text to replace, copied verbatim from the file. Must match exactly one place; include surrounding lines to make it unique.
